description:
You are working as an IT-support technician who has just received ticket **#5502** complaining that part of a small three-node database cluster is unhealthy.

    A colleague has already copied every piece of raw data you need into  
    /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/cluster_status/  

    You will find *one* “*.status” file per node:
      • node-alpha.status  
      • node-beta.status  
      • node-gamma.status  

    Every “*.status” file is a plain text file that always contains exactly **two** key–value lines:
      SERVICE=<service-name>
      STATE=<state>

    Example:
        SERVICE=db
        STATE=OK

    Your job is finished when you:
      1. Inspect every “*.status” file in the directory and work out which node(s) report  
         STATE=FAIL (there may be one, several or none).
      2. Create a brand-new log file called  
         /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/resolution.log  
         that summarises your finding in the *exact* five-line template shown below.
      3. Use no more than **three to four** ordinary shell commands (pipelines are fine) to get the job done.

    Required log-file format (five lines **exactly**; newline after each line):

        Ticket 5502 resolution summary
        ------------------------------
        Down nodes: <comma-separated-list-of-failing-nodes-or-NONE>
        Problem: Database service not running
        Action: Escalated to DB team

    Rules that will be automatically checked:

      • The file must be located at /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/resolution.log  
        (full path, correct spelling, correct extension).
      • It must contain exactly the five lines shown above, with the *only* editable
        part being <comma-separated-list-of-failing-nodes-or-NONE>.
          – If no node is down you must literally write “NONE”.
          – If one or more nodes are down, list the node names joined by a single comma and *no spaces* (e.g. “node-beta” or “node-beta,node-gamma”).
      • The file must end with a trailing newline.
      • Do not change the header lines, the dashed separator, or the two description lines.

    When you are done, the automated tests will:
      1. Read every “*.status” file in /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/cluster_status/,  
         identify which ones contain **STATE=FAIL**, and compare that set to the node list you placed on line 3 of resolution.log.
      2. Verify that resolution.log follows the exact five-line template above.

    No sudo or root access is available, so stay inside your home directory tree.

    Good luck—once resolution.log is in place and correctly formatted, ticket #5502 can be closed.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

    Directory: /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/cluster_status/

    File: /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/cluster_status/node-alpha.status
    Contents:
        SERVICE=db
        STATE=OK

    File: /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/cluster_status/node-beta.status
    Contents:
        SERVICE=db
        STATE=FAIL

    File: /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/cluster_status/node-gamma.status
    Contents:
        SERVICE=db
        STATE=OK

    Final state expected after correct completion:

    File: /home/user/tickets/ticket-5502/resolution.log
    Exact contents (including trailing newline, 5 lines total):

        Ticket 5502 resolution summary
        ------------------------------
        Down nodes: node-beta
        Problem: Database service not running
        Action: Escalated to DB team

    Permissions: default user-writable permissions (e.g. 0644) are fine.
name: task_000000_010fa6d9
